description_long: This is a tall, intricately carved stone structure reminiscent of Gothic architectural elements, likely a fragment of a larger monument or building. The surface is adorned with pointed arches, vertical columns, and ornate tracery, especially prominent near the upper section. The stone appears weathered, with some edges broken or eroded, suggesting historical age and exposure to the elements. The base is polygonal and robust, featuring subtle relief work and buttress-like projections. The upper rim is decorated with elaborate, leafy motifs and small sculptural details, giving it a highly decorative appearance. The overall color is a pale, aged stone, with some areas showing darker weathering or staining.
description: A tall, ornate stone obelisk fragment with Gothic arches, tracery, and decorative motifs, showing signs of age and weathering.
description_view_0: Front view shows the most detailed Gothic tracery and pointed arches, with a partially broken upper rim and clear vertical columns.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the depth of the relief work and the thickness of the stone, with less visible ornamentation and a more angular profile.
description_view_2: Angled view highlights the curvature of the structure and the continuity of the decorative motifs around the upper section.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays more of the vertical columns and buttress-like features, with less intricate detail than the front.
attribute_ornamentation: Extensive Gothic-style ornamentation with pointed arches, leafy motifs, and tracery concentrated near the upper section and along the vertical columns.
attribute_base_design: The base is polygonal with subtle relief work and buttress-like projections, constructed from the same stone as the rest of the structure, and appears solid and stable.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is broken and irregular, but retains some decorative leafy motifs and sculptural details along the upper rim.
